title: how to use  skopeo copy  same as "docker save SOURCE-IMAGE " to  save image to stdout ？
body:
we want save docker image to stdout stream inorder to download the image targz by web， just like

`docker save SOURCE-IMAGE | pigz -9 -p 8`

how can i use `skopeo copy`   to do this？

many thanks

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

Could you specify exactly what the source and the destination are, please? The answer is probably some combination of the `docker://`, `docker-daemon:` and `docker-archive:` (and possibly `containers-storage:`) transports, but I’m not sure what the source is, in particular.

(Also, in general, I strongly advise against the `docker save` format; it is limited and inefficient to use. A registry is almost always a better option.)
the source is  docker registry , we want to  save the docker image in docker registery into a tar.gz by http stream
@mtrmac Can u give me some guide？ thanks！

I would expect `skopeo copy docker://… docker-archive:…` to work, in general.

Just using `docker-archive:/dev/stdout` doesn’t work, because that mixes the generated archive with progress information. If the goal is to pipe the output, one way could be to use a named pipe. Or, with a bit of shell, something like
```bash
bin/skopeo copy docker://… docker-archive:/dev/fd/3 3>&1 1>&2 1>&- | tar t 
```
works for me, but the exact syntax is shell-specific.

If this approach fails in some surprising way, please provide an exact reproducer.
Got it， this is realy amazing shell ！ thanks very much！
